import io
import string
import list

fn parse_line(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut cur = 0
  mut has = false
  mut neg = false
  while i < n
    let b = match string.byte_at(s, i)
            when Some(x)
              x
            when None
              0
            end
    if b == 32 or b == 9
      if has
        if neg
          cur = -cur
        end
        result = list.push(result, cur)
        cur = 0
        has = false
        neg = false
      end
    else if b == 45
      neg = true
    else
      cur = cur * 10 + (b - 48)
      has = true
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    if neg
      cur = -cur
    end
    result = list.push(result, cur)
  end
  result
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
            when Some(s)
              s
            when None
              ""
            end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
            when Some(s)
              s
            when None
              ""
            end

let xs = parse_line(line1)
let ys = parse_line(line2)
let count = list.len(xs)
mut k = 0
while k < count
  let a = match list.get(xs, k)
          when Some(v)
            v
          when None
            0
          end
  let b = match list.get(ys, k)
          when Some(v)
            v
          when None
            0
          end
  let s = a + b
  println("#{a}+#{b}=#{s}")
  k = k + 1
end
